_description: Defines which SDLC pipeline phases execute for each JIRA issue classification. Read by the jira-classification skill to determine the execution plan. The classification result is always shown to the user for confirmation before the pipeline starts. If the user provides a different classification, that classification takes precedence.

Hotfix._description: Critical production break. Speed is the priority. No design or planning phases — go straight to implementation with an abbreviated requirements pass to capture just enough context.

Bug._description: Non-critical defect with a known root cause. Includes simplify and verification to ensure the fix does not introduce regressions, but skips architecture and design phases.

Spike._description: Time-boxed investigation to answer a specific technical question or reduce uncertainty. Produces a spike_report.md in docs/artifacts/<ticket>/ capturing findings and recommendations. No code is shipped — there is no implementation or PR phase.

Story._description: A user-facing feature or behaviour change that is well-understood enough to skip architecture. Includes implementation planning but no formal design review or risk assessment.

Small Feature._description: A contained feature that needs a design but is low enough risk to skip a formal design review and risk assessment. Full implementation lifecycle with verification. The qa-test-generation phase runs sequentially after architecture (no design-review present to parallelise with).

Large Feature._description: A significant feature with broad impact, cross-team dependencies, or architectural complexity. Runs all 10 pipeline phases including design review and risk assessment before PR creation. The qa-test-generation phase runs in parallel with design-review and is informational (does not gate Phase 4).
